products.menuLine: Pikesville Straight Rye · Kentucky · $45 · 110 proof (55.0% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: rye spice, dark fruit

guidance.bestFor: Serious rye drinker, full-bodied cigar, bonded-style strength.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when 110-proof rye should match a full-bodied cigar — spice, dark fruit, and pepper.
guidance.memberBlurb: Pikesville Rye — 110-proof Kentucky rye with dark fruit and pepper. The rye pour when Sazerac is not enough.

products.menuLine: Plantation O.F.T.D. Overproof Rum · Caribbean blend · $35 · 138 proof (69.0% ABV) · Notes: molasses, dark chocolate

guidance.bestFor: Member wants maximum rum intensity, bold cigar, tiki-adjacent strength.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the cigar is full-bodied and the rum must keep up — molasses, chocolate, 138 proof.
guidance.memberBlurb: Plantation OFTD — molasses and chocolate at 138 proof. The rum that refuses to disappear beside a ligero maduro.

products.menuLine: Redbreast 12 Year · Ireland · $65 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: sherry, baked apple

guidance.bestFor: Irish curious, pot still request, sherry-friendly cigar.
guidance.avoidIf: User wants Scotch or bourbon only.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member has not tried Irish — sherry, spice, and pot still beside a maduro.
guidance.memberBlurb: Redbreast 12 — sherry and baking spice from Irish pot still. Maybe the best whiskey category you were missing for cigar pairing.

products.menuLine: Rémy Martin XO · Cognac, France · $190 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: fig, jasmine

guidance.bestFor: Member wants elegant cognac for cigar, fruit-forward luxury, Rémy name request.
guidance.avoidIf: User wants maximum smoke or tequila only.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when silky cognac beside cigar matters — fig, plum, honey, and fine champagne finesse.
guidance.memberBlurb: Rémy Martin XO — fig, plum, and jasmine over silky oak. The cognac that whispers luxury beside a maduro instead of shouting it.

products.menuLine: Rittenhouse Straight Rye Bottled-in-Bond · Kentucky · $28 · 100 proof (50.0% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: rye spice, dark chocolate

guidance.bestFor: Rye cocktail order, spice-forward cigar, budget rye with backbone.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend for bonded rye beside a peppery cigar — spice, mint, and chocolate at 100 proof.
guidance.memberBlurb: Rittenhouse BIB — bonded rye with pepper, mint, and dark chocolate. The bartender's rye that still holds up neat beside a spicy cigar.

products.menuLine: Russell's Reserve 10 Year · Kentucky · $45 · 90 proof (45.0% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: caramel, vanilla

guidance.bestFor: Turkey fan, age-stated value, medium-full cigar.
guidance.avoidIf: User wants rye or Scotch only.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member trusts Wild Turkey DNA but wants refined 10-year oak.
guidance.memberBlurb: Russell's Reserve 10 — caramel and toffee from a decade in oak. Wild Turkey pedigree in a quieter, cigar-friendly register.

products.menuLine: Sazerac Rye · Kentucky · $32 · 90 proof (45% ABV) · Journey: Novice · Notes: rye spice, honey

guidance.bestFor: Rye curious beginner, Old Fashioned/Sazerac order, budget-friendly rye.
guidance.avoidIf: User wants 100+ proof or heavy peat.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend as the approachable rye entry — clove, citrus, and licorice without WhistlePig intensity.
guidance.memberBlurb: Sazerac Rye — clove, vanilla, and anise over pepper, citrus, and licorice. Distillery-official rye for the namesake cocktail or a neat pour.

products.menuLine: Smirnoff No. 21 Vodka · USA (multi-origin) · $16 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: neutral, light citrus

guidance.bestFor: Budget vodka cocktails, party pours, flavored-margarita base.
guidance.avoidIf: User wants premium wheat vodka character only.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend for value vodka cocktails — neutral base that will not fight a Connecticut cigar.
guidance.memberBlurb: Smirnoff No. 21 — the value vodka America still buys in volume. Neutral, mixable, and honest about what it is.
